issue_title: Move to native C++ and drop Python wrappers
issue_body:
Hi all,

Don't be affraid of such a strong issue title, I just wanted to get this discussion started about Python wrappers and performances.
It appears that using python wrappers can slow down any process using GNUradio components due to the overhead it adds.

Can this be possible, or even a good idea to move to native c++ and use Python for GUI only ?
It's not an easy thing and Python actually have important benefits to make URH cross platform, easy to deploy, etc that native c++ won't offer as this.

My knowledge about URH isn't exhautsive and I may miss some points about what really impact performances, feel free to close this if it is out of scope :)

All the best

issue_title: Functions/routines outside module are ignored in the makefile => missing reference error
issue_body:
Functions outside modules are handled just fine by normal FoBiS.py, but not in the generated Makefile.

Minimal example:
- mymod.f90
```
program main
  call print_outside_module()
end program
```
-  outside.f90
```
subroutine print_outside_module
    print*, 'outside module'
end subroutine
```

Compiling the code works just fine:

    FoBiS.py build -compiler gnu
    ./myprog

      outside module

But not in the makefile...

    FoBiS.py build -compiler gnu -m Makefile
    make clean
    make 
    
```
Compiling myprog.f90
Assembling MYPROG
obj/myprog.o: In function `MAIN__':
myprog.f90:(.text+0xa): undefined reference to `print_outside_module_'
collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status
make: *** [MYPROG] Error 1
```

I admit this is not a very clean coding style (maybe the compiler should have thrown an error in the first place...) but when working with legacy code there is not always a choice...

issue_title: TypeError: unsupported operand type(s) for *: 'NoneType' and 'float'
issue_body:
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "C:\Users\Martin\Dropbox\Code\temp\ALEX-Suite\alex\symfit_wrapper.py", line 246, in run_fitting
    fit_results.append(fit.execute())
  File "C:\Python27\lib\site-packages\symfit\core\fit.py", line 340, in execute

```

This seems to happen when a model with a lot of parameters is used, in which case the fitting fails to find cov_x and instead returns ```None```.

This is fatal error that needs to be fixed asap.

from this doc: http://arose.github.io/ngl/doc/#User_manual/Usage/Selection_language

there is no selection for atom indices?

saying I am having a series of atoms and want to make red color, how can I do the selection.

This is Amber syntax

```python
tv._add_representation('@1,2,4,10,11', 'ball+sticks', color='red')
```

The current `Expression` methodology is to have dual kinds for `Zero/One` and `Complement/Variable`, but to actually add a separate `Not` operator to negate all other operators. For example, to negate an `Or` operator, you would construct this expression: `Not(Or(...))`.

Unfortunately, adding a separate graph node for each `Not` operator bloats the size of the graph. I'm sure there's a theoretical way to describe this, but I'm guessing the data structure will tend to be about 30% bigger than it needs to be.

The solution is to get rid of `Not` operators altogether, and just make negation a part of the expression kind encoding. So for every `Or` operator, there will now be a dual `Nor` operator kind, `And/Nand`, and so on.

My proposal for a new encoding:

| Name | 4 | 3  | 2 | 1 | 0 | hex |
|:--------:|:-:|:-:|:-:|:-:|:-:|:-:|
| zero | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0x0 |
| one | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 1 | 0x1 |
| comp | 0 | 1 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0x8 |
| var | 0 | 1 | 0 | 0 | 1 | 0x9 |
| nor | 1 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0x10 |
| or | 1 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 1 | 0x11 |
| nand | 1 | 0 | 0 | 1 | 0 | 0x12 |
| and | 1 | 0 | 0 | 1 | 1 | 0x13 |
| xnor | 1 | 0 | 1 | 0 | 0 | 0x14 |
| xor | 1 | 0 | 1 | 0 | 1 | 0x15 |
| neq | 1 | 0 | 1 | 1 | 0 | 0x16 |
| eq | 1 | 0 | 1 | 1 | 1 | 0x17 |
| nimpl | 1 | 1 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0x18 |
| impl | 1 | 1 | 0 | 0 | 1 | 0x19 |
| nite | 1 | 1 | 0 | 1 | 0 | 0x1A |
| ite | 1 | 1 | 0 | 1 | 1 | 0x1B |

Observations:
* Bit 4 tells you whether the node is an atom or operator
* Bit 0 tells you whether the node is negated
* Constants start with 00, literals start with 01
* Nary operators start with 10, fixed operators start with 11
* A couple encodings left over for future enhancement, such as Majority-3
Use `requests `for all calls to OWM (currently it is only used only on certain endpoints such as Pollution API ones and Stations API endpoints).

I'm trying to use `dereference_dn` to, on a query for a user (an `inetOrgPerson`, have it derefence the `manager` attribute which points to another `inetOrgPerson`.

The `ObjectDef` looks like this:

```
object_class: ['inetOrgPerson', 'posixAccount', 'shadowAccount', 'sambaSamAccount', 'ldapProfile', 'ldapPublicKey']
    AttrDef(key='uid', name='uid'),
    AttrDef(key='name', name='cn'),
    AttrDef(key='location', name='l'),
    AttrDef(key='email', name='mail'),
    AttrDef(key='manager', name='manager', dereference_dn=object_class: ['inetOrgPerson', 'posixAccount', 'shadowAccount', 'sambaSamAccount', 'ldapProfile', 'ldapPublicKey'])
``` 

The thing I pass to the `AttrDef` as `dereference_dn` is another `ObjectDef`:

```
manager_ = ObjectDef([
        'inetOrgPerson',
        'posixAccount',
        'shadowAccount',
        'sambaSamAccount',
        'ldapProfile',
        'ldapPublicKey'
    ])
```

So the `manager` field is `AttrDef(..., dereference_dn=manager_)`.

However, when I now do a query I end up with `RecursionError: maximum recursion depth exceeded while calling a Python object`

But I don't understand why. Any DN that gets pointed at by the `manager` field can in itself have a `manager` defined on it, but since I'm not asking for that to be dereferenced in the `manager_` `ObjectDef`, how does it end up in a recursive loop like this?

issue_title: Python 3 support - 'wb' write mode causes TypeError
issue_body:
I'm trying to use Placebo in Python 3, and getting an exception `TypeError: a bytes-like object is required, not 'str'` when trying to save the API calls.

The issue is with the save method, line 60 of placebo.py: 
```python
    def save(self, path):
        with open(path, 'wb') as fp:
            json.dump(self._mock_responses, fp, indent=4)
```

The target file is being written as binary. In Python2 8-bit strings also handle byte sequences, but in Python3 byte sequences have their own `bytes` type, causing the TypeError. (https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0404/#strings-and-bytes)

@garnaat Was there a specific reason for the binary mode on the `save()` and `load()` methods? If not, the easiest fix is probably to just change them to 'r' and 'w'.

Several conditions are nullifying Leeds analysis. This is largely due to the uneven background of various images. kV imagers are performing very differently, as well as the various exposure protocols allowed. 

Non-local filters and histogram equalization would help; unfortunately these are in the skimage libary and has not been incorporated into pylinac...yet.

```python
{schema.Optional(1): 2, schema.Optional(1): 3}

schema.Schema({schema.Optional(1): 2, schema.Optional(1): 3}).validate({1:3})
```

If Optional(foo) had a hash based on foo, then Optional(const) would overwrite itself as a dict key, and you wouldn't get race condition / undefined behavior from the above construct.

Also, it would make this kind of non-sensical schema impossible:

```python
{schema.Optional([]): 2}
```

One way to achieve this would be to switch schema objects into attrs instances.
https://attrs.readthedocs.io/en/stable/

This would get "free" __eq__, __hash__, __repr__, and __init__ that behave as expected.  Also, if desired all classes could be switched to use __slots__ very easily.

This would add a dependency to schema, but might be worth it.

This breaks lines 56 and 66 in `aiohttp_stubs\.__init__.py`:

`response = MockClientResponse(method, URL(vcr_response.get('url')))`

issue_body:
An dem Thema Autostart arbeite ich gerade mit aller Macht.
Das Problem mit der neuen Installation (über setup.py) ist, dass es anders installiert wird.
Es liegt also bei den meisten unter:
/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/doorpi
/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/DoorPi-2.4.1.0.dist-info
/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/DoorPi-2.4.1.0-py2.7.egg

In der letzten Version (2.4.1.0) hab ich versucht, den home-Path des User zu bekommen, der mit dem System arbeitet:
https://github.com/motom001/DoorPi/blob/9f55bf79d76fbb39b5eb04e66b61ed05c8326a1a/doorpi/doorpi.py#L95

Also wird doorpi_cli als pi gestartet und Base_Path ordentlich aufgelöst zu /home/pi
Dort wird noch dar Name des Package dran gehangen und ergibt somit
/home/pi/DoorPi
Dort werden alle Ordner sauber angelegt (conf, media, records, ...) und ergeben folgende Struktur:
/home/pi/DoorPi/conf/doorpi.ini
/home/pi/DoorPi/conf/eventlog.db
/home/pi/DoorPi/media/ShortDialTone.wav
/home/pi/DoorPi/records/*.wav

Nun kommt das Problem - als daemon läuft es als root - das home-Verzeichnis von root kann nicht abgefragt werden und springt zurück auf /tmp/DoorPi
Dort kann aber keine Config etc liegen, da es nach dem nächsten Neustart weg ist.
Ich will aber die Pfade auch nicht hart in den Qullcode eintragen - sonst gibt es Probleme bei unterschiedlichen Distributionen und erst wenn Windows dazu kommt.
In der Config können die Pfade nicht stehen, da schon die Config in dem Pfad gesucht werden müsste.
Also müsste es theoretisch als Parameter mitgegeben werden.
Ich wollte aber keine Zwang-Parameter mehr, da mich das in Probleme bringt beim Software-Test (z.B. travis-ci)

Also drehe ich mich gerade im Kreis - wie man es macht, wird es verkehrt...

Vorschläge?

My understanding is that textblob follows pattern's implementation of sentiment analysis, which relies on a feature lexicon of adjectives (http://www.clips.ua.ac.be/pages/pattern-en#sentiment).
Is it expected behavior that when a word that can have multiple syntactic roles (not just adjective) is encountered with a different POS tag, it still will be processed as if it was an adjective?

    In []: tb = TextBlob("Reliance Jio breaks away from conservative parent firm in marketing LYF smartphone")

    In []: print tb.sentiment.polarity
    Out[]: -0.2

    In []: for w in tb.words: print (w,TextBlob(w.string).sentiment.polarity) 
    Out[]:
    ('Reliance', 0.0)
    ('Jio', 0.0)
    ('breaks', 0.0)
    ('away', 0.0)
    ('from', 0.0)
    ('conservative', 0.0)
    ('parent', 0.0)
    ('firm', -0.2)
    ('in', 0.0)
    ('marketing', 0.0)
    ('LYF', 0.0)
    ('smartphone', 0.0)

    In []: print tb.tags
    [('Reliance', u'NNP'), ('Jio', u'NNP'), ('breaks', u'VBZ'), ('away', u'RB'), ('from', u'IN'), ('conservative', u'JJ'), ('parent', u'NN'), ('firm', u'NN'), ('in', u'IN'), ('marketing', u'VBG'), ('LYF', u'NNP'), ('smartphone', u'NN')] 

The sentiment polarity of the entire sentence is driven by the term 'firm' with a weight of -0.2. However, it seems that this term is included in pattern's lexicon based on it being an adjective, while textblob parses it as a noun in this case. 
Shouldn't it be expected behavior that only tokens parsed as adjectives are looked up from the sentiment lexicon?

issue_title: Duplicate path results on goto_assignments in some cases
issue_body:
When developing, I often use editable installations for source code that I'm working on (i.e `pip install -e package_dir`). In such an environment it seems that jedi adds my source dir multiple times to `sys.path`, and this results in duplicate path results (same exact strings) on `goto_assignments` call.

Perhaps [`get_venv_path()`][1] should remove duplicate strings before returning path list?

Original issue reported in DamnWidget/anaconda#497 

[1]: https://github.com/davidhalter/jedi/blob/master/jedi/evaluate/sys_path.py#L15-L24

issue_title: bracket_start is wrong with str assignment
issue_body:
I've noticed that `bracket_start` appears to be off with the following code (`|` being the cursor):

    foo = str(|)

It returns 3, but it should be 7 (1-based).

The same applies to `foobar = str()`, where the offset should have changed, but it returns `3` for it, too.

issue_title: Add --delete-deleted option to delete branches which have been deleted from the remote
issue_body:
Whenever this error occurs running git-up:
> hotfix-write-process-output-to-console              error: remote branch doesn't exist

There is an opportunity to clean up the local branches to reflect similar clean-up performed on the remote.  --delete-deleted as an option name seems somewhat reflective of the intent.  -d is probably suitable for short.

If -d is specified, a local branch should be deleted if all of the following are true:
* The branch has an uplink configured
* There is no remote branch of the same name and/or matching the uplink
* The branch is contained in another branch
If any of the above are false, the deletion should not occur.

Note:  This is a heuristic.  It is possible to assign an uplink to a branch without ever actually using it.  This would mean the remote branch wasn't actually deleted, it was never created to begin with.  However, as long as the branch is contained in another branch, it should still be safe to delete the local reference.

Weird classification for Latvian & ex-Yougoslavian languages
issue_body:
Hi,

I noticed some weird classification problems for Latvian, the text (a subtitle) being typically classified as Luxemburgish (!). As the codes for the two languages follow each other, I was wondering whether this might be due to a bug (in the training data or the classifier)? 

Bosnian, Croatian and Serbian are also difficult to classify (these languages being very close, and until recently often classified as one single language). However, it seems that the classification of Croatian "overweights" the two other languages -- if I try to classify Bosnian and Serbian subtitles, I typically get Croatian as only possible language.
Walking through the tutorial I did not find any suggestion about running label me on window? Can you give any advice to me?

issue_title: Parameter in the uri segment matters.
issue_body:
Hi,

I faced a problem when I tried to get summary statement under paths.
Sample swagger snippet is following.

```
   /api/v1/search/:
        post:
            summary: search
            description: |
               search something
            consumes:
                - application/json
            produces:
                - application/json
            responses:
                200:
                    description: 正常終了
                500:
                    description: 異常終了

    /api/v1/{vxlan_id}/:
        get:
            summary: id check
            description: |
                Check id.
            consumes:
                - application/json
            produces:
                - application/json
            parameters:
                - name: vxlan_id
                  in: path
                  description: logical_switch_id
                  required: true
                  type: string
                  pattern: ^[a-z]{1}[a-z0-9\-_]{0,63}$
                  maxLength: 64
                  minLength: 1
            responses:
                200:
                    description: success
                500:
                    description: failure
```

When I want to know the summary statement of '/api/v1/search/',
I use match_path_to_api_path('/api/v1/search/') to get the statements under '/api/v1/search/:'.

However, match_path_to_api_path('/api/v1/search/') returns '/api/v1/{vxlan_id}/' 
because maybe '/api/v1/{vxlan_id}/' is longer than '/api/v1/search/' by 4 characters.

I don't know the specification of swagger well, but simple comparison should 
take priority over regular expression comparison, I think.

I'm not accustomed to using English, so there might be problems to understand.
In the case, please ask a question.

To solve this issue I made a PR.

Regards,

issue_title: cursor _fetch raises NoneType AttributeError on empty response
issue_body:
I am upgrading from Hive 0.10 in CDH4 to Hive 0.14 in CDH5. In this version, the attribute `TFetchResultsResp.results` can be `None` for queries that return an empty response (such as `create database` or `create table` statements). Consequently, `cursor._fetch` raises an error in [Line 222](https://github.com/BradRuderman/pyhs2/blob/master/pyhs2/cursor.py#L222) on the call to get the rows in the results attribute, which is None. This is trivially fixed with the following implementation of `_fetch` in `cursor`:
```
    def _fetch(self, rows, fetchReq):
        resultsRes = self.client.FetchResults(fetchReq)
        if resultsRes.results is None or len(resultsRes.results.rows) == 0:
            self.hasMoreRows = False
            return rows
        for row in resultsRes.results.rows:
            rowData= []
            for i, col in enumerate(row.colVals):
                rowData.append(get_value(col))
            rows.append(rowData)
        return rows
```
However, since this project is no longer maintained and it appears PRs are not reviewed, I will not be submitting a PR with the fix. I am simply filing the issue for reference. I'll (hackily) handle by catching the error and returning an empty set, though this is naturally a very poor solution to the problem.

issue_body:
I've created [a plugin to incorporate Tablesorter](https://github.com/Perlkonig/grav-plugin-tablesorter) into [Grav CMS](http://getgrav.org).

Thanks for a great fork! I was glad I found it!

While creating a Debian package for ratelimiter (as described on <https://bugs.debian.org/880661>), I found that the non-empty files in `tests/ ` are missing, and that only one of the two README files (which appear to have diverged) are provided.

Please consider adding the tests via MANIFEST.in (eg. as described [on stackoverflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/24727709/do-python-projects-need-a-manifest-in-and-what-should-be-in-it#24727824)), and having one up-to-date README that is shipped the same way.

issue_title: Do something to prevent error releasing lock in broken transaction
issue_body:
Someone should not write code like this:

```python
from django.db import transaction, IntegrityError
from django_pglocks import advisory_lock
with transaction.atomic():
  with advisory_lock("foo") as acquired:
    transaction.set_rollback(True)
    raise IntegrityError('foo')
```

Practically speaking, replace `transaction.set_rollback(True)` and the exception raised with code that produces any kind of database error.

Reason: https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/1.11/topics/db/transactions/#controlling-transactions-explicitly

> If you attempt to run database queries before the rollback happens, Django will raise a TransactionManagementError. You may also encounter this behavior when an ORM-related signal handler raises an exception.

In my environment, I can watch this in motion:

```python
In [5]: from django.db import transaction, IntegrityError
   ...: from django_pglocks import advisory_lock
   ...: with transaction.atomic():
   ...:   with advisory_lock("foo") as acquired:
   ...:     transaction.set_rollback(True)
   ...:     raise IntegrityError('foo')
   ...: 
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
TransactionManagementError                Traceback (most recent call last)
<ipython-input-5-219258098aed> in <module>()
      4   with advisory_lock("foo") as acquired:
      5     transaction.set_rollback(True)
----> 6     raise IntegrityError('foo')

/usr/lib64/python2.7/contextlib.pyc in __exit__(self, type, value, traceback)
     33                 value = type()
     34             try:
---> 35                 self.gen.throw(type, value, traceback)
     36                 raise RuntimeError("generator didn't stop after throw()")
     37             except StopIteration, exc:

/venv/awx/lib/python2.7/site-packages/django_pglocks/__init__.pyc in advisory_lock(lock_id, shared, wait, using)
     78 
     79             command = base % release_params
---> 80             cursor.execute(command)
     81 
     82         cursor.close()

/venv/awx/lib/python2.7/site-packages/django/db/backends/utils.pyc in execute(self, sql, params)
     77         start = time()
     78         try:
---> 79             return super(CursorDebugWrapper, self).execute(sql, params)
     80         finally:
     81             stop = time()

/venv/awx/lib/python2.7/site-packages/django/db/backends/utils.pyc in execute(self, sql, params)
     57 
     58     def execute(self, sql, params=None):
---> 59         self.db.validate_no_broken_transaction()
     60         with self.db.wrap_database_errors:
     61             if params is None:

/venv/awx/lib/python2.7/site-packages/django/db/backends/base/base.pyc in validate_no_broken_transaction(self)
    446         if self.needs_rollback:
    447             raise TransactionManagementError(
--> 448                 "An error occurred in the current transaction. You can't "
    449                 "execute queries until the end of the 'atomic' block.")
    450 

TransactionManagementError: An error occurred in the current transaction. You can't execute queries until the end of the 'atomic' block.
```

From my testing, the lock still gets released, I'm unclear on the exact reasons for this. Anyway, another problem is that the "foo" exception here gets lost.

Problem: It's hard for a programmer to know that they shouldn't do this.

I think that this library should do something to avoid these situations, possibilities that I can suggest:

 - Raise an error on entering the context manager if a transaction is active, tell programmer to not do that
 - If in a transaction, save the rollback state, manually perform `transaction.set_rollback(True)` then release the lock, then set the rollback state to its prior value
 - Catch `TransactionManagementError` when releasing the lock, and then do nothing (?) so that the original exception can get re-raised

I think there may still be some valid concerns about not releasing the lock, so I could see a reasonable argument that the 3rd option is a non-starter.

issue_title: mlaunch failing to connect to a standalone SSL mongod
issue_body:
I tried launching a cluster with this command: 
  mlaunch --single --dir $dir --name ruby-driver-rs \
    --wiredTigerCacheSizeGB 1 --setParameter enableTestCommands=1 --port 27400 \
    --sslMode requireSSL --sslPEMKeyFile `pwd`/cert/127.0.0.1.pem \
    --binarypath /usr/bin
It has --sslMode and --sslPEMKeyFile set. The cluster was launched, however mtools was sitting (hanging) for a long time with no output. Adding --verbose produced: 
waiting for nodes to start...
Examining mlaunch source, it looks for SSL args in: 
        for parser in self.ssl_args, self.ssl_client_args:
The two arguments I passed in are in neither of these option groups, hence it seems that the mongo client was created without SSL being set and was trying to connect to my SSL mongod via a non-SSL connection, which then wouldn't work. The following quick hack fixes this issue: https://github.com/rueckstiess/mtools/compare/develop...p-mongo:ssl-single-fix?expand=1 I'm sure there is a better way to accomplish this though. ### Expected behavior mlaunch should be able to launch a standalone SSL mongod with a self-signed certificate successfully. ### Actual/current behavior mlaunch does launch the mongod but hangs trying to connect to it. ### Steps to reproduce the actual/current behavior 
  mlaunch --single --dir $dir --name ruby-driver-rs \
    --wiredTigerCacheSizeGB 1 --setParameter enableTestCommands=1 --port 27400 \
    --sslMode requireSSL --sslPEMKeyFile `pwd`/cert/127.0.0.1.pem \
    --binarypath /usr/bin

We use smbus2 in the Mission Pinball Framework (https://github.com/missionpinball/mpf/). However, MPF is an asyncio application and most operations will block for a while. It certainly works because I2C is fast but we would like to keep latency down in the app. I looked into non-blocking mode for the smbus interface and i2c on Linux in general but i looks like that either does not work or nobody uses it. So we can probably not use select (or epoll) via the asyncio loop. Let me know if you thing that this would work.

However, I would like to have an asyncio smbus implementation anyway. Would you be up to merge a change where all ioctls would happen in an executor (a separate thread in asyncio) which synchronizes via two queues? That way the asyncio app would not have to block when performing operations and the library would only require minimal changes. What do you think?

issue_title: priors module - hypercube vector swaps entries
issue_body:
The reconstruction_for_vector in the priors module works perfectly, assigning physical values from a unit hypercube as expected to setup a reconstruction class. This includes for cases where the priors are updated after being loaded from the config file or set equal to one another - I've added tests showing this.

However, the value_vector_for_hypercube_vector still swaps the physical values that are output, if the priors are either updated manually after the initial set up or set equal to one another. This is still problematic, as the unit hypercube and physical hypercube must maintain the same order. I have added a unit test showing the problem, which I've pasted below:

```
        def test__order_maintained_with_prior_change(self):

            collection = prior.ClassMap(
                prior.Config(config_folder_path=data_path+"config_test"),
                profile_1=geometry_profiles.EllipticalProfile, profile_2=geometry_profiles.Profile,
                profile_3=geometry_profiles.EllipticalProfile)

            collection.profile_1.axis_ratio = prior.UniformPrior(100, 200)

            assert collection.value_vector_for_hypercube_vector([0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5]) == \
                   [150.0, 0.8, 0.5, 0.25, 0.5, 0.25, 0.75, 0.8, 0.5, 0.25]
```

The error is that the 150.0 entry does not appear first in the output list, but last.

I am confident I have the tools I need to fix this myself - infact I have already hacked together a solution (in the CTI code) which addresses cases like the one above - by moving the physical values to their correct entries in the list. I haven't yet solved this for settings priors equal to one another (e.g.  collection.profile_1.axis_ratio =  collection.profile_2.axis_ratio )

## Issue I tried rendering a variable without escaping HTML but got an error due to missing UTF-8 encoding. ## Example Template: `{{& content}}` Data: `{ "content": "My link with Ä german umlaut"}` Stack trace ``` Traceback (most recent call last): File "generate_site.py", line 35, in partials_ext=PARTIALS_EXTENTION) File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/chevron/renderer.py", line 232, in render def_ldel=def_ldel, def_rdel=def_rdel) File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/chevron/renderer.py", line 206, in render output += str(_get_key(key, scopes)) UnicodeEncodeError: 'ascii' codec can't encode character u'\xf6' in position 182: ordinal not in range(128) ``` ## Solution Use UTF-8 encoding for `no-escape` rendering as well. ## PR See https://github.com/noahmorrison/chevron/pull/29 Also I recommend refactoring the rendering method such that the UTF-8 encoding is done by default. This will remove the duplicate code and prevent similar errors in the future. Cheers, Lukas

issue_title: Creating multiple bots on same server instance without sharing training data
issue_body:
I've been struggling with this a bit and feel like I'm right on the cusp but keep falling back to first base. 

In the project I'm building, I want to be able to create many unique bots that can be queried using some sort of unique identifier. The bots name attribute seems irrelevant in this pursuit. Here is a simple example showing the current behavior:

./train-bots.py
-----------------------
from chatterbot import ChatBot
from chatterbot.trainers import ListTrainer

bot1 = ChatBot("Bob")
bot2 = ChatBot("Jose")

bot1.set_trainer(ListTrainer) 
bot2.set_trainer(ListTrainer) 

bot1.train(['Hi', 'Bye'])
bot2.train(['Hola', 'Adios'])
-------------------------

./bots.py
-------------------------
bot1 = ChatBot("Bob")
bot2 = ChatBot("Jose")

bot1.get_response('Hola')
OUTPUT>> <Statement text:Adios>
bot2.get_response('Hi')
OUTPUT>> <Statement text:Bye>
-------------------------
### The example works vise versa... ###

Ideally, using some sort of unique identifier, in the example I just shared I would be able to train each bot separately and persist them, so that in a following instance I could query the bot and not have it aware of other bots training data.

Is there a clean or standard way of creating bot objects that can be persisted along with their own unique knowledge graphs? I'm running my Django application with a Postgres database. My users will be communicating with bots the a REST API. So it's important that I'm able to retrieve the correct Bot in my controller (ideally something like, ChatBot.objects.get(pk=#)) where it could then be passed the incoming message and conversation_id.

Really would appreciate any thoughts or guidance on how to accomplish this (without creating a new/unique database for every new bot...).

Thank you!

issue_title: Performance: Very large arcgis ring geometry (complex, many points)
issue_body:
@chris48s: Fantastic port!!! Thanks for this project!!

I'm retrieving feature data from a government ArcGIS-based REST service and then inserting into a postgis table. I'm converting Arcgis geometry to geojson using arcgis2geojson and then using geoalchemy2 to convert to native postgis geometry (I'm sure there's a better way...)

Many users are running ALC through scheduled jobs, and likely have manually-maintained (or at least not ``*:*``) IAM policies. As such, releases that require new permissions may be problematic.

Adopt [semver](http://semver.org/) (well, officially...)

Specifically document a versioning promise around:

* Changing the names of existing services or limits is considered an incompatible API change (major version bump)
* Changing the signatures or argument types of any public (non-underscore-prefixed) methods is considered an incompatible API change (major version bump) (this seems obvious, but was done in 0.8.0 in a pretty bad way).
* Adding new limits is considered a backwards-compatible API change (minor version bump), except:
* Adding new required IAM permissions is an incompatible API change (__major__ version bump); this will likely mean that all new services, and many new limits, result in a new major version. Oh well. I'd rather announce the release of version 387 than break someone's scheduled run unexpectedly.
* Changing the version of any dependency is considered an incompatible API change
* Anything that would prevent usage like the Python examples (i.e. iterating over services and results) from working as it currently does is an incompatible API change.

issue_title: S3BotoStorage doesn't properly fallback to other Content-Type heuristics
issue_body:
Content Type is determined by the following:

1. a property called content-type
2. the filename
3. a default is used

If the `content-type` property doesn't exist, the logic falls back to 2 and 3. But the logic doesn't properly fallback if the content_type exists, but it set to None. 

I found that in django my InMemoryFileUpload had a content_type of None.  And so django-storages would try to make a s3 upload via boto3 with the following params {"Content-Type:": None}

This would cause boto to crash with 

```Invalid type for parameter ContentType, value: None, type: <class 'NoneType'>, valid types: <class 'str'>```

issue_title: compilescss breaks unfathomably if any code anywhere has invalid syntax
issue_body:
* during compilescss, every python file in the project is parsed by ast.py just in case it *might* invoke `sass_processor`
* This makes compilescss break if there is any syntax error inside any of those files
* Granted, there probably shouldn't be syntax errors in those files...
* But it's possible to have, for instance, a syntax error in a code path that's never hit in production
* These syntax errors are thus never a problem - until we run compilescss and get a syntax error.

Even worse, the error output that results is so unfathomable that #92 was misdiagnosed as being "a bug in ast.py"; as far as I can tell, it's not a bug in ast.py, it's a syntax error in some other file which would never have been noticed if we weren't asking ast.py to parse the whole file.

https://github.com/jrief/django-sass-processor/blob/c9f546d39f270a4ffa8aefabd93c328f59b891c4/sass_processor/management/commands/compilescss.py#L153

I'm not sure what to do as a permanent fix here; perhaps we can be more selective about which files to parse; or perhaps catch the syntax error and continue?

As a stopgap, I'm going to suggest a patch which would at least be more verbose about which file has the syntax error, to help make it easier to find the source of problems.

issue_title: Cache updating of tests after adding/updating results is problematic
issue_body:
Consider the following scenario:

1.  I have a newly created run, with a single test case associated with that run
2. I get a reference to the test from the run, which creates a Test object and adds the test to the `_tests` cache. At this point the test's status is `Untested`
3. I run through my scenario and decide the test has passed, creating a new Result object, associating it with the Test, and giving it a Status of Passed
4. I use the TR client to add the Result to TestRail

At this point, the Test that exists in the `_tests` cache is now outdated, as it never gets updated with the new status. If I attempt to access the test again before the cache timeout expires, I will get the test

Method `append` undefined on the **FormData** instance passed to the `formDataAppender` function.

Steps to reproduce:
  1 - Install angular-http-case-converter
  2 - Install angular-upload-file
  3 - Configure an input file as the [rails example](https://github.com/danialfarid/angular-file-upload/wiki/Rails-Example) (with the formDataAppender function)
  4 - Run the application and try to upload something

angular-upload-file version: 2.1.1
angular: 1.3.3
[angular-http-case-converter](https://bitbucket.org/laboratorium_ee/angular-http-case-converter.git): 0.1.1

**Long explanation**:

I'm using [angular-http-case-converter](https://bitbucket.org/laboratorium_ee/angular-http-case-converter.git) to normalize formats between JavaScript (camelCase) and Ruby (snake-case), so during the transformation process it creates a new object transfering the properties of the original object to the new one while converting their format.

The problem is that the object being passed when I use angular-file-upload is **FormData** and this process replaces the original object making an error occur when the request transformer calls the `append` method that doesn't exist on the new object created by the interceptor that converted the format.

Is it possible to add a costume property like `webkitdirectory` into input element?

issue_title: [Showerthought] Use virtual indexing for zero-downtime rebuilds?
issue_body:
Right now, when you rebuild a index, the index is nuked first, then rebuilt from scratch. During this reindexing process, any searches to the index might fail.

Instead, you could use "virtual indexes" to perform a rebuild without downtime. By that, I mean that you create a real index with a different name, e.g. `index_name.<timestamp>`. You can then point an alias for `index_name` and point it to the real index.

When rebuilding the index, you could create a new index in the background, populate it, then switch the aliases over. That way, the application can still use the old index while the new index is being created.

Most Elasticsearch applications I know use something like this and I'm willing to contribute something similar to this project. However, before I do that, I would like to know whether this is a desirable feature to have or whether it's unnecessary complexity for a generic library.

issue_title: Make iterating over all entries default
issue_body:
Current API behavior is to return only the first page of results (defaults to first 50 entries) when querying a set, for examples files or tasks.

```
# iterates only over first 50 files in project
for f in api.files.query(project="myprojecid"):
    print(f.name)
```

If one wants to iterate over all result entries, the `.all()` notation must be used:

```
# iterates over all files in project
for f in api.files.query(project="myprojecid").all():
    print(f.name)
```

I would say this is currently _the_ most overlooked issue for API novices. Personally, this bit me several times when I started using the API, with typically 20+ minutes debugging time because the problem does not manifest itself in any output or error log. I have also seen several other users struggling with this when they got to learn the API. In addition, this error typically shows after deployment, because developers tend to work on small sets < 50 entries. 

I know there are very good technical reasons for this behavior. But if pagination could somehow happen silently in the background without the user noticing, that would be preferred.

issue_title: Local scheduler does not prune automatically
issue_body:
Take these script: a main task that depends on a task that only completes successfully after some time has passed since launch
```python
import time
import luigi

class DepThatNeedsSomeTime(luigi.Task):
    ready_time = luigi.FloatParameter()

    def run(self):
        assert self.complete()

    def complete(self):
        return  self.ready_time <= time.time()

class MainTask(luigi.WrapperTask):
    launch_time = luigi.FloatParameter(default=time.time())

    def requires(self):
        return [DepThatNeedsSomeTime(self.launch_time + 10)]

if __name__ == '__main__':
    luigi.run()
```

run it with these configs to simplify testing/debugging:
```
python script.py MainTask --scheduler-retry-delay 1 --worker-keep-alive
```

If you run this with central scheduler, the dependency will fail initially, but after some it will be retried because the server calls `scheduler.prune` every minute:
```python
    # prune work DAG every 60 seconds
    pruner = tornado.ioloop.PeriodicCallback(scheduler.prune, 60000)
```
and `scheduler.prune` causes rescheduling/retrying of the failed task (through triggering `SimpleTaskState.prune`)
 
So eventually all work will be done successfully.


However if you run this with the local scheduler (additional `--local-scheduler`), you end up in an endless loop because `scheduler.prune` is never called and the failed task never gets a chance on a retry.

(FYI: I lost quite some time figuring all this out when I was examining the retry behaviour of Luigi with the local scheduler, assuming it would work the same as the central scheduler)

issue_title: Python-implemented COM servers broken with 64bit Python3
issue_body:
Support for implementing COM servers in Python appears to be at least partly broken when running with 64bit Python 3.6. Minimal reproducer: https://github.com/forderud/public/tree/master/comtypes-64bit .

In this reproducer, `comtypes.client.CreateObject(...)`  fails when running with 64bit Python3.6. The problem is _not_ observed when running with either 32bit Python 2.7, 64bit Python 2.7 or 32bit Python 3.6.

The problem appears to be caused by passing of a memory address as `int`. This fails with a `int too long to convert` OverflowError when calling `CopyComPointer` in 64bit.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2671400/41206281-ea52f1be-6d01-11e8-80e8-cea743a74c59.png)

I suspect that the 2nd argument to `CopyComPointer` need to be changed from `int` to a more suitable type.

issue_title: handle related managers for custom through models
issue_body:
I used a `SOFT_DELETE` policy on a "through" model for a `ManyToManyField`.  When i delete the relationship, the related object is still appearing via the related manager.

```
# models.py
from django.db import models
from safedelete.models import SoftDeleteMixin
from safedelete.models import SOFT_DELETE

class Foo(models.Model):
    bars = models.ManyToManyField('Bar', through='FooBar')

class Bar(models.Model):
    pass

class FooBar(SafeDeleteMixin):
    _safedelete_policy = SOFT_DELETE
    foo = models.ForeignKey(Foo)
    bar = models.ForeignKey(Bar)
```

Let's create one instance of each, plus a relation:

```
> foo = Foo.objects.create()
> bar = Bar.objects.create()
> foobar = FooBar.objects.create(foo=foo, bar=bar)
> FooBar.objects.count()
1
> foo.bars.count()
1
```

Now, let's delete the _relation_:
```
> foobar.delete()
> FooBar.objects.count()
0
> foo.bars.count()
1
```

Note that `foo` still thinks it is related to `bar`.  Of course, a relation exists in the database, but it's supposed to be "deleted".  i'm thinking that `foo.bars.count()` should return `0` in that case.  We would have to override the related manager for that `ManyToManyField`.

issue_title: New `strategy:value` syntax to specify locator strategy in addition to current `strategy=value`
issue_body:
Currently when specifying a non-default locator for an element, the syntax is `name=value` like in `xpath=//h1` or `css=h1`. This works well otherwise, but the same syntax is also used by [Robot Framework's named argument syntax](http://robotframework.org/robotframework/latest/RobotFrameworkUserGuide.html#named-argument-syntax) and this can cause confusion.

Using the same syntax also causes actual problems if a keyword would accept `**kwargs`, because then Robot considers all arguments looking like `name=value` to match it. There's also a possibility that some time in the future Robot will always do that, regardless on the function signature. Robot allows escaping the problematic `=` like in `name\=value` (e.g. `css\=h1`) but that is a bit ugly if needed often.

My proposal is that in addition to the existing `=`, we allow using `:` like `name:value` (or `name: value` or even `name : value` as we allow spaces to used with `=` too). The reason I propose `:` is that it's visually quite close to `=`, it's used by Robot on the command like (e.g. `-v name:value`), and it's also used by Python in similarly as `=` in some contexts (e.g.`{'name': 'value'}` and `dict(name=value)`). We don't need to deprecate the current syntax at least now, but documentation should be written so that the new syntax is recommended.

This change causes backwards incompatibility problems if someone has used a default locator (e.g. id) which has `:` in its value `Click Element    foo:bar`. We don't expect `:` to be too common in locator values, and there's always a possibility to use `id:foo:bar` or `id=foo:bar` to avoid the problem. If there are lot of complaints during the SL 3.0 release candidate phase, we can still consider ways to make the transition smoother.

issue_title: Tables should be located using same logic as other elements
issue_body: Currently table related keywords have their own logic for finding tables, and this logic doesn't support all the same locator strategies and syntax than the logic for finding other elements. This means the usage of keywords isn't consistent and that the functionality needs to be separately documented. Better to change table locating logic to use the same logic that is used for locating other elements.

issue_title: bin/upgrade finds running instances using hardcoded instance*
issue_body:
According to "bin/upgrade help":

    The Zope instance is discovered automatically by searching for all
    "zope.conf" files in "parts/instance*" of the buildout directory, looking
    up the instance port and testing whether the port is bound on localhost.

In our buildouts we use 'zeoclient' as the instance name in a zeoclient/zeoserver setup.

Can we somehow change this hardcoded search for instance* using a parameter for bin/upgrade or otherwise add a URL parameter for bin/upgrade to specify the instance to which to connect?

I am trying to test minio-py on ubuntu

$ sudo pip install minio

After that i am trying to list all my buckets: with my secret/access key

```
#!/usr/bin/env python

from minio.minio import Minio

# Instantiate a client
client = Minio('https://s3.amazonaws.com',
                access_key='MYKey',
                secret_key='MYKey')

# List buckets
buckets = client.list_buckets()
for bucket in buckets:
    print 'bucket:', bucket.name, bucket.creation_date

I am getting this error:

ubuntu@ubuntu:~/python$ python listB.py

▽
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "listB.py", line 11, in <module>
    buckets = client.list_buckets()

    raise SSLError(e)
urllib3.exceptions.SSLError: [Errno 1] _ssl.c:510: error:14090086:SSL routines:SSL3_GET_SERVER_CERTIFICATE:certificate verify failed
```

Right now ``extruct.extract`` has an ``url`` parameter which is documented as "url of the html documents", but in reality it's used as a base url (at least in ``LxmlMicrodataExtractor``, maybe in others as well). I think we should check if it's indeed always used as a base url, update documentation and introduce ``base_url`` argument deprecating ``url``? Another option would be to extract ``base_url`` in extruct, but this feels like worse solution to me (what if caller already has base_url or has more accurate base_url?), although we could also support both base_url and url.

issue_title: Always set default value for maxBoost to 1.0
issue_body:
As discussed by @subutai on [HTM Forum](https://discourse.numenta.org/t/why-maxboost-is-always-2-0/560/7?u=rhyolight):

> maxBoost is described in the Spatial Pooler pseudocode [1]. As Alex Lavin mentions at the end of his post, boosting is, by definition, only useful for really long and complex data sources where you need to optimally allocate the SP columns. For most simple streaming tasks it can be set to 1.0.

> To my knowledge, swarming does not explore different values of boosting, just uses a default value of 2.0. I think that default should be set to 1.0. There is an existing NuPIC ticket [2] to improve the various swarm parameters, and this should be one of them. There are other parameters that could be improved in swarming.

> [1] http://numenta.com/assets/pdf/biological-and-machine-intelligence/0.4/BaMI-Spatial-Pooler.pdf
> [2] https://github.com/numenta/nupic/issues/2829

@hachreak @jirikuncar

This RFC is late since work on it has already started. Apologies for that. It's however just small wrapper around existing features such as ``format_record`` and ``perform_request_search`` so hopefully it will not be hard to get consensus.

**Proposal**
Create a new Records REST API which supports *getting* records and *searching* records. Additionally make the API flexible enough to be easily extended in the future if we would like to add batch uploading, files, comments, ... 

 * The basic REST *resource* is a ``record``, which internally is just represented via Invenio records. 
 * A record resource can have many different *representations*: ``application/json``, ``application/marcxml+xml``, ``application/mods+xml``, ``application/x-bibtex``, ``application/x-datacite+xml``, ``text/html`` just to name a few. 
 * Each *representation* (mime type) is produced by an *output format*. 
 * An overlay can configure which output format is used for each mime type, allowing an overlay to have a custom representation for e.g. ``application/json``. By default ``application/json`` will be produced by the ``recjson`` output format.

Additional features:
* Support both unauthenticated and authenticated access to API.
* Rate limiting (via Flask-Ratelimiter), with default limits for unauthenticated requests, and configurable limits per OAuth2 application.
* Pagination support

**URL design**

* GET Record
  * GET ``/api/records/:id`` where ``:id`` is the Invenio record id.
  * Request headers: ``Accept: <mime type>`` e.g. ``Accept: application/x-bibtex``. If not specified ``application/json`` is assumed.
  * Query parameters: None (but we could have something like ``?fields=title,author`` to limit the fields returned, but this would mainly make sense for ``application/json`` and be hard to implement for e.g. DataCite.
  * OAuth Scope required: guests (none), authenticated requests (``records:read``)
  * Error codes: 400, 403, 404, 410 (for deleted records), 415 (unsupported media type), 429 (rate limiting)
* LIST Records (i.e. search)
  * GET ``/api/records``
  * Request headers: ``Accept: <mime type>``. We'll have to think of how we can return output formats like e.g. DataCite which doesn't have the concept of collections. Do we just concatenate the results?
  * Query parameters: Two options exists
     1. Keep friends from ``perform_request_search`` such as ``p, c, cc, sf, rm ...``. Pagination however ``jrec`` and ``rg`` however will need to be implemented according to previous RFC #1724.
     2. (preferred) Take the chance to refactor the parameters to something a bit more user friendly and map them to ``perform_request_search``. E.g. something like ``query``, ``sort_field``, ``sort_order``.
  * Pagination: Like per previous RFC #1724 
    * Error codes: 400, 403, 415 (unsupported media type), 429 (rate limiting)


The URL design allows for possible future extensions such as:
```
# Relations from one record to other objects.
/api/records/:id/files/
/api/records/:id/comments/
# REST Subcollections for e.g. collections or communities (note return result is records in the given collection).
/api/records/collections/:collection_id/
```

**Open questions**:
How do we handle access records via multiple identifiers? By default a resource should only be available on a single URL, so alternative URLs could just redirect to the canonical URL. E.g.:

``/api/records/doi/10.5281/zenodo.1 -> /api/records/1``

or 

``/api/records/doi:10.5281%2fzenodo.1 -> /api/records/1``

or 

``/api/persistentid/doi/10.5281/zenodo.1 -> /api/records/1``

Do we have examples from other REST APIs how they deal with this?

Co-authored-by: @konstantinoskostis, @jirikuncar, @egabancho, @tiborsimko

issue_title: Inconsistency in environment vs environments in API

Environments, and all list items, are referenced in the API in plural form. For example, the following url lists environments and is at /environment**s**: https://qa-reports.linaro.org/api/environments/

Is this a bug or what am i missing? thanks anyway!

this line in the serializer causes turtle output to lose datatype info
https://github.com/RDFLib/rdflib/blob/master/rdflib/plugins/serializers/turtle.py#L304

```
from rdflib.term import Literal
from rdflib.namespace import XSD
print(Literal(10, datatype=XSD.decimal)._literal_n3(use_plain=True))
print(Literal(10, datatype=XSD.decimal)._literal_n3(use_plain=False))
```
results in:
```
10.0
"10"^^<http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#decimal>
```

in the first scenario the round trip loses the info that the value is a decimal with no '.0', simply '10'

although support for xsd:gYear was added in #636 it appears to interpret things improperly:
(schema details here: https://www.w3.org/TR/xmlschema-2/#gYear)

```python
Literal('2002', datatype='http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#gYear')
```
return:
```python
rdflib.term.Literal('2002-01-01', datatype=rdflib.term.URIRef('http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#gYear'))
```
and
```python
Literal('2002-01', datatype='http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#gYearMonth')
```
returns:
```python
rdflib.term.Literal('2002-01-01', datatype=rdflib.term.URIRef('http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#gYearMonth'))
```

issue_title: 没有自己的 Exception 类型
issue_body: 一个建议：定义自己的异常类型，在抛异常时抛自己的，并精细错误检查，而不是直接 catch Exception。这样可以把实际出现的异常描述的更清楚，客户端也更容易使用。从库设计的角度看，暴露下层细节（比如 requests），一般也不是太好的做法。

issue_title: [gtk3] "ls -l" output underlined, should disallow space in filename
issue_body:
(Even with Quick Open disabled)

Execute `ls -l` and mouseover an entry that is newer than half year (showing hh:mm instead of year), e.g.

    drwxrwxrwt 16 root root 20480 Jan 25 01:19 tmp

Notice that up to (including) the timestamp it's all underlined.

I believe that the second regex of Quick Open assumes that this is a file named "drwxrwxrwt 16 root root 20480 Jan 25 01", and line 19.

Ctrl+click also prints silly things.

I think an easy and nice workaround could be to remove space from the allowed characters of filenames.

issue_title: Handling lines in pdfminer Plane may require modifications
issue_body:
In #7, we significantly reduced the size of this repository by cutting `pdfminer` out of our repo and simply installing it as a dependency. Unfortunately, in the `extract_tables` utility, it appears that we needed modifications to the pdfminer library itself. Specifically, if we use `pdfminer.six` as is, we get this error:

```
[INFO] __main__ - Processing 1-s2.0-S003101829800159X-main.pdf (50 of 132)
ERROR:pdftotree.pdf.pdf_parsers:(184.194, 184.194, 50)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/lwhsiao/repos/pdftotree/pdftotree/pdf/pdf_parsers.py", line 68, in cluster_vertically_aligned_boxes
    plane.extend(boxes)
  File "/home/lwhsiao/repos/pdftotree/.venv2/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pdfminer/utils.py", line 341, in extend
    self.add(obj)
  File "/home/lwhsiao/repos/pdftotree/.venv2/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pdfminer/utils.py", line 346, in add
    for k in self._getrange((obj.x0, obj.y0, obj.x1, obj.y1)):
  File "/home/lwhsiao/repos/pdftotree/.venv2/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pdfminer/utils.py", line 334, in _getrange
    for x in drange(x0, x1, self.gridsize):
  File "/home/lwhsiao/repos/pdftotree/.venv2/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pdfminer/utils.py", line 171, in drange
    assert v0 < v1, str((v0, v1, d))
AssertionError: (184.194, 184.194, 50)
```

when we try and extend the Plane with a box like 
```
<LTTextLineHorizontal 184.194,683.116,184.194,693.800 '(cid:211)\n'>]
```

This assertion is modified in the [original TreeStructure repo](https://github.com/HazyResearch/TreeStructure/blob/3bdd143358f3ec24d040c3aaa42fbbd2d10ab7aa/table-extraction/pdfminer/utils.py#L121-L125)

```py
def drange(v0, v1, d):
    """Returns a discrete range."""
    assert v0 <= v1
    return xrange(int(v0)//d, int(v1+d)//d)
```
to be `<=` rather than just `<`. There are likely other changes that were made to pdfminer itself in the TreeStructure repository. We should try and understand those changes, and see if it's possible to avoid them so that we don't have to have a duplicate of that library in our repo.


## Reproducing

On the `sen_ml` branch, you can run
```
extract_tables --train-pdf data/paleo/ml/train.pdf.list.paleo.not.scanned --gt-train data/paleo/ml/gt.train --test-pdf data/paleo/ml/test.pdf.list.paleo.not.scanned --gt-test data/paleo/ml/gt.test --datapath data/paleo/documents/ --model-path data/model.pkl -vv           
```

to see the error.

Something like:

```python
async with FirefoxSession(*some, **options) as firefox:
    await firefox.get('http://example.com')
``` 

what do you think?

issue_title: Pip freeze to requirements.txt creates chalice deploy error on Python 3.6.1
issue_body:
Steps to reproduce. Python 3.6.1 installed via homebrew on Mac OS 10.12.6, although the error seems to be reproducible with Python 2.7 as well:

1. Create a new python3 virtual environment and install chalice
$python3 -m venv ./env
$source ./env/bin/activate
$pip install chalice
(which installs typing as a dependency)

2. Create a new chalice project and deploy
$chalice new-project freeze-test
$cd freeze-test
$chalice deploy
(deploys correctly as expected)

3. Freeze current requirements and redeploy
$pip freeze > requirements.txt
requirements.txt is now:
 
```
botocore==1.6.5
chalice==1.0.1
click==6.6
docutils==0.14
jmespath==0.9.3
python-dateutil==2.6.1
six==1.10.0
typing==3.5.3.0
```

$chalice deploy

During deploy, this error shows up:

```
Could not install dependencies:
typing==3.5.3.0
You will have to build these yourself and vendor them in
the chalice vendor folder.

Your deployment will continue but may not work correctly
if missing dependencies are not present. For more information:
http://chalice.readthedocs.io/en/latest/topics/packaging.html
```

…then it hangs updating the lambda function.
With chalice —debug deploy, it hangs at:

`botocore.endpoint - DEBUG - Sending http request: <PreparedRequest [PUT]>`

after write errors like:

```
2017-08-23 08:31:27,251 - botocore.endpoint - DEBUG - ConnectionError received when sending HTTP request.
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "{$Path}/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/botocore/vendored/requests/packages/urllib3/connectionpool.py", line 544, in urlopen
    body=body, headers=headers)
  File “{$Path}/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/botocore/vendored/requests/packages/urllib3/connectionpool.py", line 349, in _make_request
    conn.request(method, url, **httplib_request_kw)
  File "/usr/local/Cellar/python3/3.6.1/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.6/lib/python3.6/http/client.py", line 1239, in request
    self._send_request(method, url, body, headers, encode_chunked)
  File "{$Path}/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/botocore/awsrequest.py", line 130, in _send_request
    self, method, url, body, headers, *args, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/local/Cellar/python3/3.6.1/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.6/lib/python3.6/http/client.py", line 1285, in _send_request
    self.endheaders(body, encode_chunked=encode_chunked)
  File "/usr/local/Cellar/python3/3.6.1/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.6/lib/python3.6/http/client.py", line 1234, in endheaders
    self._send_output(message_body, encode_chunked=encode_chunked)
  File "{$Path}/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/botocore/awsrequest.py", line 157, in _send_output
    self.send(msg)
  File "{$Path}/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/botocore/awsrequest.py", line 242, in send
    return HTTPConnection.send(self, str)
  File "/usr/local/Cellar/python3/3.6.1/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.6/lib/python3.6/http/client.py", line 986, in send
    self.sock.sendall(data)
  File "/usr/local/Cellar/python3/3.6.1/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.6/lib/python3.6/ssl.py", line 965, in sendall
    v = self.send(data[count:])
  File "/usr/local/Cellar/python3/3.6.1/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.6/lib/python3.6/ssl.py", line 935, in send
    return self._sslobj.write(data)
  File "/usr/local/Cellar/python3/3.6.1/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.6/lib/python3.6/ssl.py", line 636, in write
    return self._sslobj.write(data)
socket.timeout: The write operation timed out
```

Once the deploy gets into this bad state, nothing that I've tried short of deleting the app gets it deploying again, like removing the typing requirement from the file or removing the requirements.txt file altogether. 

Although the error references the typing module, removing typing from the frozen requirements.txt will still produce the error. Removing both typing and chalice from the frozen requirements, leaving a file like:

```
botocore==1.6.5
click==6.6
docutils==0.14
jmespath==0.9.3
python-dateutil==2.6.1
six==1.10.0
```
...will not produce the issue.

I'm very new to Chalice, so hopefully this isn't something idosyncratic about my environment. I should be able to work around this by freezing only specific packages. Thanks!

I am using Chalice version 1.6.0. I have 100 MB of python packages in the `/vendor` directory, because of the `matplotlib` that my app depends on. The zipped deployment package is 30 MB, below the 50 MB AWS package size limit. 

My ISP's upload speed is low, slightly below 1 mbps. The deployment takes longer than 5 minutes and fails with the following message:

> ChaliceDeploymentError: ERROR - While sending your chalice handler code to Lambda to update function 
"create_report-dev-create_report", received the following error:
>  An error occurred (InvalidSignatureException) when calling the 
 UpdateFunctionCode operation: Signature expired: 20180727T161712Z is now 
 earlier than 20180727T162142Z (20180727T162642Z - 5 min.)

This I think, is due to the low tolerance of v4 signature. Is there a way around it? Is another signing / authentication method is available? Or, can chalice create timestamp at a later stage to save time?

issue_title: DISCUSSION: Is there a way for a Transaction to indicate success/failure from __exit__
issue_body:
From #336.

Currently, there is no way to determine if this occurs. It's unclear what a way forward would look like but in `ndb` transactions have [retries][1] and can [create][2] `Future` objects which know about the success or failure.

[1]: https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/python/ndb/transactions
[2]: https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/python/ndb/functions

issue_title: BigQuery: Add support for BigQuery customer-managed encryption keys
issue_body:
BigQuery customer-managed encryption keys allow users to specify a Cloud KMS key to protect their BigQuery table.

API:
Jobs: https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/reference/rest/v2/jobs
In a job, there is a destinationEncryptionConfiguration field, which indicates which Cloud KMS key should be used for the destination.

Tables: https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/reference/rest/v2/tables
In a table, there is a encryptionConfiguration field, which indicates which Cloud KMS key protects (or should protect in case of CreateTable) a BigQuery table.

These are the main APIs that are required for day-to-day interaction.
With lower priority, support for getServiceAccount would also be nice: https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/reference/rest/v2/projects/getServiceAccount
Note that unlike the other methods mentioned above, this would generally only be called once and the resulting value (the email address) does not change - so it can easily also be called from UI/API/CLI without much hindrance (hence lower priority).

issue_title: Datastore: receiving duplicate entities during query iteration
issue_labels: [{'id': 77676109, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/api:%20datastore', 'name': 'api: datastore', 'color': 'f8f8f8', 'default': False}, {'id': 544604244, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/priority:%20p1', 'name': 'priority: p1', 'color': 'ffa03e', 'default': False}, {'id': 663697852, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/status:%20investigating', 'name': 'status: investigating', 'color': 'fef2c0', 'default': False}, {'id': 77438580, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/type:%20bug', 'name': 'type: bug', 'color': 'db4437', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
Hi.

I'm using google-cloud-datastore==1.3.0 from pypi.

When iterating over a query iterator the google-cloud-datastore sometimes returns the same entitiy twice.

I've had a look at the source and it seems that the last entity of a fetched page is the same as the first entity of the next page.

Iterating of pages and printing each enitity id makes the problem obvious.
```python
query_iter = qry.fetch()
pages = query_iter.pages
while True:
    page = next(pages)
    if not page:
        return

    for ent in page:
        print(ent.key.id)

    print("-- new page --")
```

The output looks like this:
```
6438744212111360
5774037759295488
5226622299930624
6390563805855744
5537056630505472
5007404484788224
5289011263307776
5805078561685504
-- new page --
5805078561685504
4785039263924224
4655690686660608
5253444773347328
5481464721309696
```

As you can see the entity 5805078561685504 is returned twice.

Dumping the protocol buffer shows that the 'cursor'-value of the last entity and is identical to the 'end_cursor'-value. Here are the last lines of the dump:```

```
      }
    }
  }
  cursor: "\n\346\002\n\260\002\n\003ka2\022\250\002\032\245\002..."
  version: 1507954620963000
}
end_cursor: "\n\346\002\n\260\002\n\003ka2\022\250\002\032\245\002..."
more_results: NOT_FINISHED
```

Maybe you can help.

issue_body:
This error can be reproduced by:

1. Deploying the following stack:
```
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'

    Default: logs.us-west-2.amazonaws.com

    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'

```

```
namespace: stackertesting

stacks:

    template_path: templatefile.yaml
```

```
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'

    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'

            Action: 'sts:AssumeRole'

      ServiceNameTwo: logs.us-west-2.amazonaws.com
```
3. Deploy the updated stack interactively:
```
 $ stacker build -i -r us-west-2 config.yaml 
[2018-08-07T15:45:47] Using interactive AWS provider mode.

issue_title: Add new params to AWS::EC2::VPCEndpoint type
issue_body:
On June 21st, 2018, AWS added CloudFormation support for the following params:
- Use the PrivateDnsEnabled property to indicate whether to associate a private hosted zone with the specified VPC.
- Use the SecurityGroupIds property to specify the ID of one or more security groups to associate with the endpoint network interface.
- Use the SubnetIds property to specify the ID of one or more subnets in which to create an endpoint network interface.
- Use the VpcEndpointType property to specify the type of endpoint.

[CloudFormation Release History](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/ReleaseHistory.html)
[AWS::EC2::VPCEndpoint CloudFormation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/aws-resource-ec2-vpcendpoint.html)

Hey there,

I just spent a bit of time with AWS Support, so wanted to pass it on and get a type check in Route.

troposphere will gladly generate a template from this code:

```python
nat = t.add_resource(ec2.NatGateway(
    'NatGateway{}'.format(letter),
    AllocationId=GetAtt(eip, 'AllocationId'),
    SubnetId=az['subnet_id']
))
t.add_resource(ec2.Route(
    'NatGateway{}Route'.format(letter),
    GatewayId=Ref(nat),
    DestinationCidrBlock='0.0.0.0/0',
    RouteTableId=Ref(route_table_id),
))
```

and silly me didn't realize that `GatewayId` is only for IGWs and VGWs. For a NAT Gateway they have a separate field called `NatGatewayId` when creating a Route. The above should be:

```
t.add_resource(ec2.Route(
    'NatGateway{}Route'.format(letter),
    NatGatewayId=Ref(nat),
    DestinationCidrBlock='0.0.0.0/0',
    RouteTableId=Ref(route_table_id),
))
```

I think some troposhere resources do some type checking, right? Can we add a type check to `GatewayId` in `Route` to return an error message when someone references a `ec2.NatGateway`?

issue_title: ProjectTree reports files in OS-specific orders
issue_body:
ProjectTree `scandir` currently reports files in an OS/fs-specific order, as evidenced by https://rbcommons.com/s/twitter/r/4121/ : this will likely lead to `Snapshot` ID mismatches across hosts, and other nasty inconsistencies.

I think that the right fix would be to provide an ordering guarantee at the ProjectTree level by sorting the resulting entries, and then validate/enforce it in [scan_directory](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/engine/fs.py#L256).

I mentioned this in a thread on pants-devel and in a discussion in https://rbcommons.com/s/twitter/r/1979/

I would like to propose a 'Recipes' documentation page where you mention how to use Pants to solve different problems.  Initially I would like to contribute "recipies" for:

-  developing with a local jar file,

- configuring a maven repo to live side by side with pants.  

This kind of document would be good not just for showing how to do things, but for expressing some opinions about the relative merits of these configurations too because they all seem to have their own controversies.

issue_title: Create a Single Page App Kolibri Module Subclass
issue_body:
## Summary

The vast majority of frontend code in Kolibri is accessed via module that acts as the driving Single Page App for that particular URL endpoint.

These SPAs all define the following functionality:
- URL Routes under their Django defined entry points
- Instantiating a Vuex Store for client side state management
- An entry component that acts as an initial render point and controller for the SPA

As such, I propose we create a SinglePageApp subclass of the KolibriModule class that implements the following functionality:
- Router initialization based on a `routes` hash
- Store initialization based on the core store module and the SPA's own store (ideally, this would depend on #2022, and not actually require the initialization of the store to be delegated to the SPA, rather the SPA would register these new Vuex modules against a store that has already been instantiated in the core app - using the Vuex2 functionality that allows for dynamic registration of modules).
- Initializing and attaching the entry component

## Summary

Exercises not rendering on IE9.

## Error

```
ReferenceError: 'btoa' is undefined 
```

## How to reproduce

1. View an exercise on IE9.

## Screenshots

issue_title: Exabgp sends messages with multiple MP_(UN)REACH_NLRI attributes
issue_body:
When using flowspec announcements or withdraws we have noticed that when sending ipv4 and ipv6 flowspec rules exabgp will group both families into a single update message by using two MP_REACH or MP_UNREACH path attributes one for each family.

According to Section 5 of RFC4271 this is invalid
> The same attribute (attribute with the same type) cannot appear more
> than once within the Path Attributes field of a particular UPDATE
> message.

When testing this on a Juniper MX router it sends us a notification message back about malformed attribute list. The cisco ASR9k we tested just seems to ignore the second MP_REACH attribute.

[exabgp-malformed-batched-mpreach-nlris-example.pcap.gz](https://github.com/Exa-Networks/exabgp/files/571873/exabgp-malformed-batched-mpreach-nlris-example.pcap.gz)

The [documentation](https://docker-py.readthedocs.io/en/stable/images.html) for Images::pull states the usage as: pull(name, **kwargs). However, its Parameters description does not have 'name' but just has 'repository' (which used to work with the older low-level api client)

Looking at the code, the docker-py/docker/models/images.py has the function declared as:
def pull(self, name, **kwargs) and passes name, kwargs to the docker-py/docker/api/image.py:: ImageApiMixin::pull whose parameter is named repository (and not name).

The consequence is:
**client.images.pull(repository=<repository_details>)** fails with TypeError: pull() takes exactly 2 arguments (1 given) since it expects name though as per the documentation, repository should work. 

client.images.pull(name=<name>, repository=<repo_details>) fails with TypeError: pull() got multiple values for keyword argument 'repository' as the name parameter was passed in to the next method which interprets it as repository. 

client.images.pull('image_name') - works - and works for a name that has registry / tag in it. 

Given that under the wraps, we are accepting a repository format for this API, Can we just rename the only mandatory parameter as repository (from current 'name'). This would be consistent with the older behavior and to be consistent with the push api - and more in line with the actual default behavior when no parameter name is specified.

issue_title: Crash in `_deep_copy_contours` when a glyph includes a non-existent component
issue_body:
I had this problem while building a Glyphs font with `fontmake -g XXXX.glyphs -i`. The test font happens to have glyphs that point to non existent components. This crash happens now that https://github.com/googlei18n/glyphsLib/issues/317 has been fixed in https://github.com/googlei18n/glyphsLib/pull/329. glyphsLib warns about the problem as such:

```
INFO:fontmake.font_project:Building master UFOs and designspace from Glyphs source
INFO:glyphsLib.classes:Parsing "../../../Downloads/XXXX.glyphs" file into <GSFont>
WARNING:glyphsLib.builder.builders.UFOBuilder:Anchors not propagated for inexistent component circumflexcomb_acutecomb in glyph acircumflexacute

Aug 09 19:17:27 ip-10-21-1-251.ec2.internal systemd[1]: livenet-blue_v3.web.1.service: control process exited, code=exited status=1
Aug 09 19:17:27 ip-10-21-1-251.ec2.internal systemd[1]: Failed to start livenet-blue_v3.web.1.

```

deis `v1.7.3`

The machine it is starting on still seems to have memory. The image is relatively large though this appears to be RAM related?

```
core@ip-10-21-1-251 ~ $ free -m
             total       used       free     shared    buffers     cached
Mem:          7517       7456         60         22        516        700
-/+ buffers/cache:       6239       1277
Swap:            0          0          0

core@ip-10-21-1-251 ~ $ docker -v
Docker version 1.5.0, build a8a31ef-dirty
```

It could be useful if you could configure process types to run in particular places based on fleet metadata with deis tags.

For example, if you have asynchronous workers that don't necessarily have to be on a fast machine but you want your web processes on better hardware.  Or you have asynchronous workers which require a lot of CPU/Memory and you want to schedule them only on hardware that could support it.

Right now, you have to have separate apps for them which can lead to a lot of redundancy, multiple pushes/envirionment config, etc.

I understand not everyone is running a heterogeneous cloud environment but there is some aspect of cluster cost maintenance untapped here where you could have redundant large hosts paired with redundant smaller hosts and reserve the large hosts for process types that need them.  Case in point here from a deis core perspective, the deis-builder.

issue_title: project seems dead,here may have a better choice
issue_body:
https://github.com/rasbt/mlxtend
A library of extension and helper modules for Python's data analysis and machine learning libraries.

suport stacking for  Regression and Classification

```
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.gridspec as gridspec
import itertools
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
from sklearn.svm import SVC
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
from mlxtend.classifier import EnsembleVoteClassifier
from mlxtend.data import iris_data
from mlxtend.plotting import plot_decision_regions

# Initializing Classifiers
clf1 = LogisticRegression(random_state=0)
clf2 = RandomForestClassifier(random_state=0)
clf3 = SVC(random_state=0, probability=True)
eclf = EnsembleVoteClassifier(clfs=[clf1, clf2, clf3], weights=[2, 1, 1], voting='soft')

# Loading some example data
X, y = iris_data()
X = X[:,[0, 2]]

# Plotting Decision Regions
gs = gridspec.GridSpec(2, 2)
fig = plt.figure(figsize=(10, 8))

for clf, lab, grd in zip([clf1, clf2, clf3, eclf],
                         ['Logistic Regression', 'Random Forest', 'RBF kernel SVM', 'Ensemble'],
                         itertools.product([0, 1], repeat=2)):
    clf.fit(X, y)
    ax = plt.subplot(gs[grd[0], grd[1]])
    fig = plot_decision_regions(X=X, y=y, clf=clf, legend=2)
    plt.title(lab)
plt.show()
```

### What is wrong?

I let Trintiy run over night, trying to sync mainnet, came back to just getting tons of errors about Too many open files:

```
OSError: [Errno 24] Too many open files
   ERROR  05-30 10:58:52  base_events  socket.accept() out of system resource
socket: <socket.socket fd=15, family=AddressFamily.AF_INET, type=2049, proto=6, laddr=('0.0.0.0', 30304)>
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/asyncio/selector_events.py", line 179, in _accept_connection
    conn, addr = sock.accept()
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/socket.py", line 205, in accept
OSError: [Errno 24] Too many open files
   ERROR  05-30 10:58:52  base_events  socket.accept() out of system resource
socket: <socket.socket fd=15, family=AddressFamily.AF_INET, type=2049, proto=6, laddr=('0.0.0.0', 30304)>
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/asyncio/selector_events.py", line 179, in _accept_connection
    conn, addr = sock.accept()
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/socket.py", line 205, in accept
OSError: [Errno 24] Too many open files
```

### How can it be fixed

I don't know...

Implement some APIs for shard chain validators to call the `validator manager contract` on main chain.

* contract code: https://github.com/ethereum/sharding/blob/develop/sharding/contracts/validator_manager.v.py
* previous implementation (with pyethereum): https://github.com/ethereum/sharding/blob/develop/sharding/validator_manager_utils.py

Implementation should probably remain *low* level.  A simplistic `Contract` class with the following API should suffice.

* Base class with `factory` class method which accepts `abi` and returns a `ContractClass`
* `ContractClass` as returned by `BaseContract.factory` can be instantiated with a single `address`.
* A method on the `ContractClass` for constructing a transaction (TODO: should this be a dictionary, a `Message` class, or something else?)

There are likely some other convenience APIs that should be added around this functionality to make common tasks easier as we find what those are.

I think this code should live in something like `evm.utils.contracts`.

    import sys
